name: android-deface
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``android-deface`` project. Tool in the Mobile Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with darklotuskdb/frida-scripts-fetcher-exploiter (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with codprg/mobile (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with cloudfuzz/android-kernel-exploitation (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web']' as the attack surface (mobile_security, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Mobile Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Mobile Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Mobile Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
